element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Keyboard/joystick controls established before complex combat
Players learn to outfit up to three squadmates per mission
Players gather weapons, equipment, and metal from destroyed Hercs and buildings
Players learn chassis properties, armor, shield output, speed, and equipment mounting constraints
Players discover mission failure doesn't always end campaign; varying degrees of success unlock different paths
New weapon types unlocked through mission success rewards
Reward for specific mission victories; provides access to additional hardpoints and capabilities
Final confrontation with Prometheus; must be won or campaign ends

typical_relief_triggers: Mission briefing and debriefing sequences (video format)
Salvage collection and inventory management between missions
Herc customization and loadout configuration phases
Successful mission completion with varying degree of success
typical_tension_triggers: Base defense objectives under coordinated attack
Branching mission paths with permanent consequence potential
Terminal mission sequences with no retry option
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Good pacing with fast action punctuated by strategic customization phases; tension escalates through branching mission structure where early decisions affect later encounters

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Different Herc chassis available with varying hardpoint configurations allow players to engage differently with same mission space

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory missions establish basic piloting and tactical squad management without overwhelming complexity
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Mission variety increases with branching paths; consequence system introduces strategic depth; salvage-based progression requires resource optimization
difficulty_curve.late_game: Terminal mission sequence on Luna with no alternative paths; final confrontation with Prometheus requires mastery of all accumulated systems
notable_spikes: Mission branching system creates variable difficulty paths based on prior success/failure
Introduction of terminal missions removes retry option, raising stakes
Final Luna sequence escalates from North America/South America/Antarctica/Asia/Australia/Europe operations to off-world confrontation

notable_level_patterns.description: Missions requiring protection of multiple bases under attack; partial failure acceptable (loss of one or two bases doesn't end mission); success degree determines mission branching outcome
Destroyed enemy units and buildings leave behind metal, weapons, and equipment added to player inventory; strategic looting informs subsequent mission loadout choices
Mission failure typically opens alternative campaign branches rather than ending game; only terminal missions (final Luna sequence) have irreversible consequences
Successful mission completion rewards new weapon types, equipment pieces, or new Herc chassis; some equipment inaccessible if specific missions failed
